name: blocks
context: Notion block types — paragraphs, headings, lists, toggles, callouts, code, embeds
info: Blocks are the atomic content units in Notion. Every piece of content inside a page is a block: paragraphs, headings (h1/h2/h3), bulleted lists, numbered lists, to-do items, toggles, callouts, code blocks, quotes, dividers, tables, images, bookmarks, embeds, and more. Blocks can be nested — toggles contain child blocks, list items can have sub-items, and columns organize blocks side by side. The Blocks API supports retrieving, updating, deleting, and appending children. All text content uses the rich text object format with annotations for bold, italic, strikethrough, code, color, and links.
triggers: what block types does notion support
add content to a notion page
add a code block to notion

sections.title: Block types
sections.body: Notion supports paragraph, heading_1/2/3, bulleted_list_item, numbered_list_item, to_do, toggle, callout, quote, code (with language), divider, table/table_row, image (external or file), bookmark, embed, equation, column_list/column, and more. Each type has a specific object shape in the API. Code blocks accept a `language` field. Callouts accept an `icon` (emoji or external URL). Images accept `external: { url }` or `file: { url }`. See [[notion/pages#content-blocks]] for using blocks in page creation.

sections.title: Nesting blocks
sections.body: Blocks that support `has_children` can contain child blocks. Toggles are the primary nesting mechanism — their children are hidden until the toggle is expanded. List items nest to create indented sub-lists. Headings can be toggled to show/hide their children. Column lists contain column blocks, which in turn contain content blocks. Use `notion.blocks.children.list()` to retrieve children and `notion.blocks.children.append()` to add them. See [[notion/pages#content-blocks]] for initial block structure.

sections.title: Append children
sections.body: Use `PATCH /v1/blocks/{block_id}/children` or `notion.blocks.children.append({ block_id, children })` to add blocks. The `block_id` can be a page ID (append to page) or a block ID (append to that block's children). Use the `after` parameter with a sibling block ID to insert at a specific position. Retrieve existing children with `GET /v1/blocks/{block_id}/children` or `notion.blocks.children.list()`. Update a single block with `PATCH /v1/blocks/{block_id}`. Delete with `DELETE /v1/blocks/{block_id}`. Be mindful of [[notion#rate-limits]] when performing bulk block operations.

sections.title: Rich text format
sections.body: Rich text is an array of objects, each with `type` (text, mention, equation), content, optional `link`, and `annotations` (bold, italic, strikethrough, underline, code, color). Colors: default, gray, brown, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple, pink, red, plus background variants (gray_background, etc.). Mentions reference pages, databases, users, or dates inline. Equations use KaTeX syntax. Maximum 2000 characters per rich text element. See [[notion/pages#properties]] for rich text in page properties.
